Functions for initializing and controlling the RTC with options for fast and slow modes.

# Purpose
This code is a C++ header file that provides a narrow set of functions for managing a Real-Time Clock (RTC) in an embedded system. The function `rtc_init` initializes the RTC using the Low-Speed External (LSE) oscillator as the clock source and returns a boolean indicating the success of the initialization. The function `rtc_initialize_fast_mode` configures the RTC to operate in a fast mode. The function `rtc_slow_down` adjusts the RTC to a slower speed to maintain timekeeping during standby mode. The function `rtc_speed_up` increases the RTC speed to support firmware operations.
